question:
Will the price of Ethereum be above $4,300 on November 6?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance ETH/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

Underlying (e.g., ETHUSDT, BTCUSDT) index stream for all symbols. Updates every 1000ms. evidences.title: Ethereum Price Forecast & Predictions for 2026, 2027, 2028–2030 ...
evidences.content: Many analysts expect ETHUSD to maintain bullish momentum in the second half of 2026. According to some forecasts, the price could reach $2,582.92–$2,792.54, while more conservative estimates point to a rise toward $2,423.35. Forecasts for 2027 are more mixed. Most analysts expect the uptrend to continue, with ETH potentially reaching $2,526.50–$3,061.81. More cautious projections suggest a target of around $2,231.26. Most analytical platforms also anticipate continued positive price action between 2028 and 2030. Some forecasts indicate that ETH could climb to $3,187.35–$3,233.90 by the end of 2030, while others believe the price is unlikely to exceed $2,810.11. [...] #### Changelly Price range (USD): 1,717.49–2,825.27. Changelly analysts also maintain a bullish outlook for ETH in the second half of 2026. A short-term pullback to the $1,853.21–$1,945.50 range is expected in September. evidences.title: Getting Historic Crypto Prices with Binance API | by Ziwon Kim
evidences.content: ## Understanding the Binance API We will use Binance’s Kline/Candlestick Data API endpoint. It takes 5 parameters and gives 12 return figures including OHLCV explained below. ### Input Parameters symbol — BNBUSDT interval —“1m” representing 1 minute startTime —UTC in ms. Will be set dynamically (ex. 1636710431000) endTime — Start from midnight yesterday and go back by 13 days each limit — 1000 for maximum efficiency Press enter or click to view image in full size Image 5 Screenshot of sample API response from Binance ### Output Returns (for BNBUSDT symbol by minute) [...] ### Output Returns (for BNBUSDT symbol by minute) Open Time & Close Time —Different by minute -1microsecond OHLC— Price information in USDT Volume — Volume in BNB Number of trades Quote asset volume — Volume in USDT Taker buy base asset volume — Volume in BNB of Taker Buy(=Maker Sell) —_i.e._ v _olume = “taker buy base asset volume” + “taker sell base asset volume” => We can also get Taker Sell / Maker Buy / Maker Sell in BNB_ Taker buy quote asset volume — Volume in USDT of Taker Buy Ignore — literally ignore ## CODE ### Initial Setup First, set the fixed variables including the cryptocurrency symbols you want...

evidences.title: Market Data endpoints | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: ### Kline/Candlestick data​ `GET /api/v3/klines` Kline/candlestick bars for a symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. evidences.title: Ethereum price today, ETH to USD live price, marketcap ...
evidences.content: ### Ethereum Shanghai Upgrade The biggest Ethereum upgrade since The Merge, the Shanghai Upgrade will allow ETH stakers to unstake their ETH and withdraw ETH rewards from the Beacon Chain. During The Merge, the Ethereum proof-of-work chain merged with the proof-of-stake Beacon Chain. Instead of mining, validators stake 32 ETH to secure the network. However, stakers are unable to unstake and withdraw until the Shanghai Upgrade. The Shanghai/Capella (“Shapella”) Upgrade is a hard fork that will implement five EIPs — the most anticipated being EIP-4895, which will enable withdrawals. Shanghai is the hard fork’s name on the execution layer, while Capella is the name on the consensus layer. evidences.title: Simple Green/Red Candle Trading Strategy for ...
evidences.content: What Is the Green/Red Candle Strategy? The Green/Red Candle Strategy uses the color of candlesticks on a 1-hour (H1) or 15-minute (M15) chart to determine whether to buy or sell. It’s designed for cryptocurrency pairs with strong liquidity and volatility, such as BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT, or BNB/USDT, and can be implemented on platforms like Binance, Bybit, or KuCoin. Traders select three daily trading times based on their local time zone, aligning with active market sessions (Asian, European, and U.S.) to maximize opportunities. Key Features Instruments: Crypto pairs like BTC/USDT, ETH/USDT, BNB/USDT. Trading Times: Three daily times in your local time zone, aligned with: Asian Session: Early morning (e.g., 08:00-10:00 local time). [...] Candle: H1 closes green (+$200) Action: Buy at $60,000 SL: $59,800 (low of prior candle, $200 risk) TP: $60,400 (1:2 ratio, $400 profit) Outcome: Price hits $60,400, yielding profit; or hits $59,800, incurring a small loss. Trade 2: ETH/USDT (H1, Afternoon Session) Location: London, UK (12:00 BST) Candle: H1 closes red (-$150) Action: Short at $59,500 SL: $59,650 (high of prior candle, $150 risk) TP: $59,200 (1:2 ratio, $300 profit) Outcome: Price dr...

evidences.title: Secret weapon "Mastering Time Zones" | Trade Oracle X ...
evidences.content: ## 🔥 The Golden Overlaps: Where Magic Happens Time zone overlaps are where liquidity and volatility collide, creating prime trading windows. Here’s how to ride them on Binance: ### 1. Tokyo-London Overlap (UTC 08:00–10:00) 🇯🇵🇬🇧 What’s Happening? Asian traders hand off to Europe, creating a moderate-volume sweet spot. Why It’s Lit: Early Asian trends solidify or reverse as European liquidity kicks in. Perfect for catching momentum. Binance Play: Use Binance Spot for quick scalps on ETH/USDT or BNB/USDT. Check Binance Square for crossover sentiment. Example: A breakout above a key resistance on ADA/USDT at UTC 09:00 could signal a trend. Enter with a tight stop via Binance’s OCO orders. ### 2. London-New York Overlap (UTC 13:00–16:00) 🇬🇧🇺🇸 [...] ### 1. Asian Session (UTC 00:00–08:00) 🌸 Who’s Trading? Japan, South Korea, China, and Southeast Asia dominate. Think Binance’s massive APAC user base. What’s Hot? Pairs like BTC/JPY, ETH/USDT, and BNB/USDT see heavy action. Altcoins often pump first here. Why It Matters: Asian markets often set the daily trend. News like China’s regulatory moves or South Korea’s exchange hacks can spark wild volatility. Pro Tip: Follow Binance Square for...

evidences.title: does the stock market follow daylight savings time?
evidences.content: Timestamps and logs: If systems store local timestamps without timezone metadata, DST transitions can create duplicate or skipped times (e.g., the hour that is repeated when clocks fall back). Always store timestamps in UTC and convert to local time only for presentation or scheduling. Order scheduling: Orders set for a local clock time (e.g., a limit order to activate at 08:00 local) may effectively trigger an hour earlier or later in exchange time when DST changes. Brokers or platforms that translate exchange hours to user local time should warn users around transitions. Algorithmic strategies: Time‑based rules (e.g., open‑only execution, intraday rebalancing) must reference exchange local time or UTC, not the trader’s desktop clock, to preserve behavior across DST changes. [...] Verify broker display: Confirm that your broker (for crypto and equities, Bitget) shows exchange hours in both exchange-local and your local time, and note any DST flags. Use UTC internally: When automating orders, use UTC or exchange local time (with explicit timezone) so scheduled activity is consistent across DST transitions. Review scheduled orders and alerts: If you set any order to trigger at a...
